overview.short: Bijasan Mata Temple is one of the temples of the Hindu goddess Durga, situated in Indore District of Madhya Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Bijasan Mata Temple is one of the temples of the Hindu goddess Durga, situated in Indore District of Madhya Pradesh, India. This temple is built in the year 1760 by Shivaji Rao Holkar. This temple is visited by thousands of devotees during Navaratri.

topPlaces.name: Sinhasa
topPlaces.description: Sinhasa is a census town in Indore district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Gangwal Bus Stand
topPlaces.description: The Gangwal Inter-state bus terminus popularly known as Gangwal Bus Stand or ISBT, located in Indore is one of the oldest and one of the biggest Inter State Bus Terminals in Indore. It operates bus services between Indore (Madhya Pradesh) and 2 other states, Rajasthan, Gujarat.

topPlaces.name: Indore
topPlaces.description: Indore ( ; ISO: Indaura, Hindi: [ɪn̪d̪ɔːr]) is the largest city of the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh where it serves as the capital and the administrative headquarters of the eponymous district and division. Modern-day Indore was established on the banks of the Kanh and Saraswati rivers and traces its roots to its 16th-century founding as a trading hub between the Deccan Plateau and Delhi.

topPlaces.name: Rajwada
topPlaces.description: Rajwada, also known as the Holkar Palace or Old Palace, is a historical palace in Indore that was constructed by the Holkars of the Maratha empire around 2 centuries ago. An example of the architecture of the time, the palace is a 7-story structure that is placed near the Krishnapura Chhatri.

topPlaces.name: Lalbagh Palace
topPlaces.description: Lalbagh Palace was built by the Holkar Dynasty, and is situated in Indore, Madhya Pradesh, India. The Holkar family resided in the palace up to 1978.

topPlaces.name: 2025 Indore drinking water contamination
topPlaces.description: 2025 Indore drinking water contamination was a public health crisis that occurred in Bhagirathpura, a locality in Indore, Madhya Pradesh, India. At least 15 people died, and approximately 270 others were hospitalised after the municipal drinking water became contaminated, resulting in a widespread outbreak of waterborne disease.

topPlaces.name: Kanh River
topPlaces.description: The Kanh is a river flowing through Indore, the largest city in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. The river started carrying sewage in the early 1990s.

topPlaces.name: Malwa
topPlaces.description: Malwa (IPA: [maːlʋaː]) is a historical region of west-central India occupying a plateau of volcanic origin. Geologically, the Malwa Plateau generally refers to the volcanic upland north of the Vindhya Range.

items.note: Sinhasa is a census town in Indore district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.

The Gangwal Inter-state bus terminus popularly known as Gangwal Bus Stand or ISBT, located in Indore is one of the oldest and one of the biggest Inter State Bus Terminals in Indore. It operates bus services between Indore (Madhya Pradesh) and 2 other states, Rajasthan, Gujarat.

Indore ( ; ISO: Indaura, Hindi: [ɪn̪d̪ɔːr]) is the largest city of the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh where it serves as the capital and the administrative headquarters of the eponymous district and division. Modern-day Indore was established on the banks of the Kanh and Saraswati rivers and traces its roots to its 16th-century founding as a trading hub between the Deccan Plateau and Delhi.

Rajwada, also known as the Holkar Palace or Old Palace, is a historical palace in Indore that was constructed by the Holkars of the Maratha empire around 2 centuries ago. An example of the architecture of the time, the palace is a 7-story structure that is placed near the Krishnapura Chhatri.
Lalbagh Palace was built by the Holkar Dynasty, and is situated in Indore, Madhya Pradesh, India. The Holkar family resided in the palace up to 1978.

2025 Indore drinking water contamination was a public health crisis that occurred in Bhagirathpura, a locality in Indore, Madhya Pradesh, India. At least 15 people died, and approximately 270 others were hospitalised after the municipal drinking water became contaminated, resulting in a widespread outbreak of waterborne disease.
The Kanh is a river flowing through Indore, the largest city in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. The river started carrying sewage in the early 1990s.

Malwa (IPA: [maːlʋaː]) is a historical region of west-central India occupying a plateau of volcanic origin. Geologically, the Malwa Plateau generally refers to the volcanic upland north of the Vindhya Range.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bijasan mata mandir indore?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bijasan mata mandir indore is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bijasan mata mandir indore?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Devi Ahilya Bai Holkar Airport, Indore (1 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhopal Junction (175 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bijasan mata mandir indore?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bijasan mata mandir indore at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bijasan mata mandir indore?
faq.a: Stays in Bijasan mata mandir indore start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bijasan mata mandir indore?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sinhasa, Gangwal Bus Stand, Indore, Rajwada, Lalbagh Palace.
